title: Pollo a la Gallina Tava
description: Tender and flavorful Peruvian chicken dish made with dark roasted chicken, brown sugar, and aji amarillo peppers. This recipe is perfect for a hangover cure.

ingredients.name: Chicken legs (4-6)
ingredients.notes: Bone-in and skin-on for maximum flavor.

ingredients.name: Aji amarillo peppers (4-6)
ingredients.notes: Substitute with jalape472 or other hot peppers if needed.

ingredients.name: Brown sugar
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality pure cane sugar for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground for the best flavor.

instructions: Preheat the tava or griddle to medium-high heat.
Season the chicken with salt and black pepper, then drizzle with olive oil.
Add the aji amarillo peppers to the tava or griddle and cook until slightly charred, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger to the tava or griddle and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Place the chicken on the tava or griddle and cook until browned on both sides, about 5-7 minutes per side.
Transfer the chicken to a plate and let rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Meanwhile, mix the brown sugar and lime juice in a small bowl until well combined.
Brush the sugar and lime mixture onto the chicken during the last 2 minutes of cooking.
Garnish with chopped cilantro, if desired.
